title: galactia striata var. berteroana
intro: Galactia striata var. berteroana, also known as Mexican milk pea or Bertero's milkpea, is a climbing vine native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central America, with delicate white or pink flowers and a history of use in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. In herbal medicine, Galactia striata var. berteroana is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce pain and swelling. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
